text:
MADISON, Wis. (WMTV) - A Sun Prairie author was recently featured in a New York Times list of children’s books with Olympic themes.

Pat Zietlow Miller is the author of over 20 children’s picture books, and her book “The Quickest Kid in Clarksville” was recently featured inThe New York Times.

The book, illustrated by Frank Morrison, is about a little girl in 1960′s Clarksville, Tennessee who dreams of being the fastest girl alive - just like her hero, Wilma Rudolph, who made history as the first women from the U.S to win three gold medals in one Olympics.

The girl is trying to attend Wilma’s welcome back party after she won her medals with another local girl, and along they way they find they may have more in common then they think

Zietlow said the book focusses on perseverance, just like Wilma had when she was told she may never walk again after a childhood polio diagnosis, but also reminds readers of that well-known feeling of being a young kid inspired by an athlete.

“You see a lot of times like on social media even now people are watching like Simone Biles and there are little, tiny girls trying to do the flips that she does and so I just think with the Olympics it’s just a great time for kids to think well maybe I could do that,” Zietlow said.

Pat Zietlow is a firm believer that picture books are not just for kids and encourages people of all ages to read her books.

title: ‘The Quickest Kid in Clarksville’ shines in New York Times’ Olympic children’s books list
description: A Sun Prairie author was recently featured in a New York Times list of children’s books with Olympic themes.
keywords: olympics,wilma rudolph,the quickest kid in clarksville,pat zietlow miller,childrens books,sun prairie
